title: E20 petrol row: Govt answers 9 FAQs on mileage, performance, damage, pricing & more
content: The government has said that the Ethanol Blended Petrol (EBP) Programme is a major component of India's energy strategy, designed to strengthen energy security, support the farming community and lower environmental impact by increasing the use of domestically produced renewable fuel.

title: 'Lost my son & dad': Ketan's father writes to President, seeks fast-track justice
content: Vishal Agarwal has appealed to President Murmu for a swift investigation. He seeks a fast-track court hearing for his son Ketan's murder case. The family suffered immense grief after losing both son and father recently. Ketan was allegedly murdered by his fiancée and her friend. The investigation into this tragic incident is currently ongoing.

title: 'Just a shame': RCB star reveals Kohli was secretly photographed during private meet
content: England batter Jordan Cox shared disappointment regarding Virat Kohli's privacy issues.  Secret photographs of the former India captain surfaced online after a London meeting.  Cox noted that hundreds of people passed by without interruption during their catch-up.  He expressed sympathy for Kohli's inability to live a normal life away from cricket.  Cox also praised Kohli's selfless nature and mentorship during their IPL stint.

title: Monsoon session: NDA in command, opposition in disarray. Will DMK tilt the balance?
content: The opposition parties are grappling with significant turmoil following vital splits that have diminished their strength in Parliament. Recent shifts have bolstered the ruling NDA coalition's standing. As the Monsoon session approaches, the potential reintroduction of a Constitution Amendment Bill looms. Internal fractures within several regional parties could further compromise the opposition's capacity to impede crucial legislation.

title: No salary for me until we achieve AGI: MiniMax CEO makes a promise to its employees
content: MiniMax CEO Yan Junjie will not take a salary until artificial general intelligence is achieved. He plans to transfer personal shares to reward employees and support open-source AI development. The company is seeking to raise approximately $2.05 billion through a Hong Kong share placement. These funds will expand AI infrastructure and accelerate research and development efforts. MiniMax aims to bolster its position amidst increasing competition in China's AI market.

title: Watch: Sachin, hailed as 'perhaps the greatest batsman of all time', spotted at Wimbledon
content: Sachin Tendulkar attended the Wimbledon men's singles semifinals on Friday, where commentators hailed him as "perhaps the greatest batsman of all time." The cricket legend watched British wild card Arthur Fery face French Open champion Alexander Zverev on Centre Court. A regular Wimbledon visitor, Tendulkar once again drew attention as one of the event's high-profile guests.

title: Mike Commodore accuses Edmonton Oilers amid Mike Babcock controversy
content: Former NHL defenseman Mike Commodore has stirred the pot around the hockey world after saying the Edmonton Oilers sent him a threatening email as he continued to criticize head coach Mike Babcock. The claim has only added more heat to the ongoing public feud between Commodore and Babcock and sparked a conversation about social media wars involving NHL personalities and teams.

title: Melinda Gates to students: When a woman steps into power, it is better for all
content: Melinda French Gates received the Josef Korbel Award for her philanthropic work. She stressed that empowering women builds stronger families and nations. French Gates highlighted the importance of centering women in philanthropic efforts. Her organization Pivotal invests in entrepreneurs and advocates for paid leave. She urged students to contribute their skills to local communities.

title: Rare British butterfly with yellow wings is unlike any other, DNA reveals
content: A new study confirms the British swallowtail butterfly is genetically unique. This native species possesses significantly less genetic diversity than European relatives. Researchers found no accumulation of harmful mutations despite its isolation. However, higher inbreeding levels pose challenges for its long-term survival. Genomic research offers vital insights for protecting vulnerable butterfly populations.

title: Quantum experiment raises a strange question: What if time isn’t real?
content: In a groundbreaking experiment, physicist Giovanni Barontini sought to decipher the origins of time by creating a mini-universe through a Bose-Einstein condensate close to absolute zero. In this unique setting, events analogous to the Big Bang and Big Crunch unfolded, while an innovative entropic clock monitored the natural progression of disorder, indicating that time may be an illusion arising from complex internal dynamics.

title: Heat of Europe's AC ban comes to New York; Mayor Mamdani shares AC tips
content: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani urged residents to reduce electricity use. He asked people to adjust AC settings and limit unnecessary power consumption. The city is also implementing similar measures in government buildings. Officials aim to reduce pressure on the power grid during the heat wave. This appeal comes as electricity demand rises across New York City.

title: IAF ranks 3rd in global airpower, outflanks China for 5th time since 2022
content: The Indian Air Force secured third place globally in the 2026 WDMMA rankings. This ranking places India's air power ahead of China's People's Liberation Army Air Force. The World Directory of Modern Military Aircraft uses a proprietary True Value Rating system. This system evaluates air forces based on fleet size and composition. India's air force is undergoing modernization and procurement programs to enhance capabilities.

title: How China can bring Taiwan to its knees without firing a shot
content: China may employ a gray zone strategy to isolate Taiwan economically and psychologically.  This approach involves naval, cyber, and information operations below war thresholds.  A blockade would restrict vital imports, impacting Taiwan's energy and supply chains.  Cyberattacks and disinformation campaigns would further destabilize the island's society.  Taiwanese resilience drills prepare for a multifaceted siege beyond conventional military conflict.

title: Palak Purswani Wedding: Bigg Boss OTT 2 actor weds Rohan Khanna; shares dreamy photos
content: On June 26, Palak Purswani and Rohan celebrated their love in a serene ceremony at the Isha Yoga Centre in Coimbatore. Set against the majestic backdrop of the Adiyogi statue at dawn, their vows were exchanged in the company of cherished family and friends. Dressed in a stunning beige and red lehenga, Palak beamed alongside Rohan, who sported a classic sherwani.

title: Bankipur bypoll twist: BJP candidate set to face Prashant Kishor withdraws
content: In a surprising turn of events, a Bharatiya Janata Party candidate has stepped down from the Bankipur bypoll, citing family reasons. The party has swiftly chosen Neeraj Kumar Sinha as the new candidate. This change paves the way for Prashant Kishor's highly anticipated first appearance in the electoral arena, as he prepares to contest against the BJP's stronghold in this area.

title: 'He became very emotional': Vijay meets Karur stampede victims' families
content: In a heartfelt gesture, Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay visited families affected by the Karur stampede disaster. He distributed job appointment orders to eligible dependants as part of rehabilitation efforts, visibly moved by their immense sorrow. This visit coincided with the High Court's evaluation of the government's permanent job appointment policies. Furthermore, Vijay criticized the opposition DMK for allegedly exploiting the tragedy for political gain.

title: 'I feel the time has come': Iyer's captaincy questioned after England series loss
content: New T20I captain Shreyas Iyer faces scrutiny after two series defeats. Former selector Saba Karim urges Iyer to assert his captaincy identity. Karim questioned batting order decisions, specifically Shivam Dube's placement at number five. He noted this differed from Iyer's IPL tactical approach. Leadership and communication are crucial for captains in pressure situations.

title: Pinaka to Suryastra: India's push for deeper, precise strikes without fighter jets
content: India's Operation Sindoor demonstrated precision strikes against terror targets deep inside Pakistan. The Pinaka rocket system has evolved to hit targets up to 120 kilometers away. New variants like the Long Range Guided Rocket enhance India's strike capabilities significantly. Suryastra is envisioned as a next-generation system to rival missile accuracy. These developments signify India's shift towards non-contact warfare and enhanced deterrence.

title: 'Tastes like cough syrup': Telangana techie's wife sent text hours before death in US
content: A husband faces murder charges after his wife's death was initially reported as sudden. Raajitha Sabbineni's last message about a bitter smoothie became key evidence. Autopsy results revealed she died from strangulation, contradicting the initial account. Investigators uncovered evidence of the husband's alleged affair with another woman. He was arrested in Bellevue, Washington, four months after their wedding.

title: 48-hour miracle: How Delhi just did something it hasn't done in 3 years
content: Delhi's air quality reached the 'good' category for the first time in nearly three years. Continuous monsoon rains washed pollutants away, lowering the Air Quality Index significantly. This event highlights how quickly weather can cleanse the city's atmosphere. However, maintaining this improvement remains a significant environmental challenge for Delhi. Experts emphasize that year-round emission reduction policies are crucial for sustained clean air.

title: 'It's stupid': Norway fan refuses iconic WC Viking Row celebration, explains why
content: As Norway cheers for its football team's remarkable journey to the World Cup quarter-finals, fans have adopted a rowing celebration that reflects their Viking roots. This captivating sync has turned heads during the tournament, yet one vigilant fan finds fault with the tribute, suggesting it's a misrepresentation of history and too closely resembles Iceland's beloved clap. His muted protest serves to challenge the celebration's genuine connection to Viking legacy.

title: How loitering munitions are making battlefields more lethal and unpredictable
content: Loitering munitions are enhancing India's military capabilities significantly. These systems combine surveillance and strike functions, reducing target engagement times. India's army is integrating these drones to supplement existing artillery and missile systems. Dedicated drone warfare units are being established, reflecting growing confidence in their effectiveness. Indigenous production and foreign integration position India for future battlefield dominance.

title: Remove addictive features or pay fine: EU tells Meta to fix Instagram, Facebook
content: The European Commission has charged Meta Platforms with breaching EU digital law.  Instagram and Facebook features are accused of keeping users hooked and addicted.  Regulators demand changes or face significant fines for the company.  Meta rejects the allegations, citing improved teen protections and parental controls.  The company will continue engaging constructively with European Union regulators.

title: 60 bullets fired, 4 gangsters dead: Encounter that exposed Deepak Nandal's crime empire
content: Haryana Police recently engaged in a fierce confrontation with the Deepak Nandal gang, during which four suspected shooters were killed and a kidnapped businessman was successfully rescued. Deepak Nandal, the gang's leader, operates from outside the country and is known for his involvement in extortion and contract killings across northern India. Authorities are intensifying efforts to unravel his international criminal connections.

title: Kerala medical student killed over 'forced conversion' claims in Uzbekistan
content: A Kerala medical student was murdered in Uzbekistan by a fellow student. The victim allegedly faced repeated demands to convert her religion before her death. Uzbek authorities have arrested the accused student in connection with the ongoing investigation. Indian authorities are assisting the victim's family and liaising with Uzbek officials. A second post-mortem was conducted in Kerala after the body's repatriation.

title: How UDAN 2.0 plans to get India's regional aviation dreams past the turbulence - explained
content: India's UDAN scheme aims to make flying affordable for all citizens. The initial phase connected many smaller cities but faced sustainability challenges. A modified UDAN scheme now focuses on airport development and broader ecosystem support. This new phase includes increased financial aid and indigenous aircraft integration. The government hopes this will create a self-sustaining regional aviation network.

title: 4 burnt alive in car, 7 die fleeing on foot in deadly Spain wildfire
content: At least eleven people died after a wildfire swept through southern Spain overnight.  Authorities reported four victims were found inside a vehicle near the mountains.  Seven others died after abandoning their cars and attempting to flee on foot.  Spain has long grappled with devastating wildfires exacerbated by strong winds and heat.  The nation experienced record-breaking heat in June, contributing to excess deaths.

title: PM Modi gifts Steve Waugh rare framed photo during MCG visit
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi visited the Melbourne Cricket Ground with Australian PM Anthony Albanese, gifting Steve Waugh a framed photo from their meeting nearly 20 years ago. The leaders unveiled a sports cooperation roadmap and backed stronger sporting ties. Albanese also announced Chennai will host the first-ever Big Bash League match outside Australia during the 2026-27 season.

title: Rajpal Yadav gets no relief in Rs 9 crore debt case; lawyer makes fresh claims
content: Actor Rajpal Yadav has been sentenced to three months imprisonment by a Delhi court. This decision comes after he failed to repay a significant loan amount. The court dismissed all cases filed by Rajpal Yadav, upholding the previous verdict. He has also been directed to pay a substantial fine in each case. Yadav has been given two months to comply with the court's order.

title: Reverse oil flow: India supplies gasoline to Russia - why it matters
content: Indian refiners are well equipped from a product-quality standpoint to supply the gasoline grades required by Russia. However, Oil minister Hardeep Singh Puri has said that Indian companies are not directly selling gasoline to Russia.

title: 29-year-old Smriti Mandhana scripts history, becomes youngest ever to ...
content: Smriti Mandhana celebrated her 300th international appearance during India's historic women's Test at Lord's, becoming the youngest woman to reach the milestone. She reflected on overcoming doubts after the 2017 World Cup and said Lord's was the perfect venue for the occasion. Mandhana also stressed moving on from setbacks and returning to basics while preparing for red-ball cricket.

title: China's mega dam on Brahmaputra facing major natural threat: Report
content: The warning comes at a time when Beijing's massive dam project has already become a geopolitical flashpoint. India has repeatedly expressed concerns over China's upstream activities on the Brahmaputra, citing potential risks to water security, ecology and downstream communities. The latest study now raises fresh questions over whether the project also carries significant geological risks acknowledged by Chinese scientists themselves.

title: 'Polygamy practitioners to lose govt jobs': Assam's budget proposes strict rules
content: Assam's government proposed barring polygamous individuals from state welfare schemes.  Government employees practicing polygamy may face dismissal from their service.  The state also plans to amend existing rules for disciplinary actions.  This initiative aims to promote women's empowerment and gender justice.  The government will resume its welfare schemes from August onwards.

title: 'We will definitely try to sort it out': BCCI to review Team India after England tour ends
content: The Board of Control for Cricket in India will review the men's T20I team's performance. This detailed review will commence immediately after the ongoing England tour concludes. The team has faced significant challenges during their recent white-ball matches. Problems will be identified and addressed following the series' conclusion. Team India aims to rectify issues and improve future team performances.

title: Engineering student got no Wi-Fi, bad food in hostel; court orders Rs 30K refund
content: A district consumer commission directed a Greater Noida hostel to refund Rs 30,000. The student paid an advance for accommodation and promised facilities. The hostel failed to provide good food and working Wi-Fi. The commission found this a deficiency in service under the Consumer Protection Act. The hostel must also pay interest, compensation, and litigation costs.

title: Beyond building homes: How Odisha is combining affordable housing with rooftop solar
content: Odisha is revolutionizing housing schemes by incorporating rooftop solar systems, allowing beneficiaries to acquire subsidized solar panels for their new homes. This initiative is designed to lower long-term electricity costs for low-income families, making housing more affordable. Additionally, it encourages the adoption of clean energy, thus broadening the benefits of green housing to a greater segment of the population.

title: 'I'll do everything in my power to ... ': Salah reacts to Egypt's World Cup exit
content: Egypt captain Mohamed Salah shared an emotional message with fans after their World Cup exit. He promised to do everything in his power for Egyptian football's international success. The Pharaohs achieved a historic World Cup journey, topping their qualifying group. They secured their first knockout victory against Australia in a penalty shootout. Their dream run concluded with a narrow defeat against Argentina in the Round of 16.

title: 'Critical 3rd parties': UK puts Microsoft, Google, Amazon, Oracle under oversight
content: Major cloud providers like Microsoft and Google are now critical third parties.  This designation brings their cloud operations under direct regulatory oversight.  The new framework aims to safeguard the country's financial system resilience.  This move addresses increasing reliance on cloud services by financial firms.  The regulations take effect on July thirteenth, enhancing financial stability.

title: Actor Rajpal Yadav gets 3 months jail as HC upholds conviction in cheque bounce cases
content: The Delhi High Court, led by Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma, has sentenced Bollywood actor Rajpal Yadav to three months of imprisonment in each of seven cheque bounce cases stemming from a Rs. 9 crore debt dispute. Rejecting any immediate relief for the actor after a failed settlement process, the court ordered Yadav to pay Rs. 1.05 crore to the complainant per case, alongside fine adjustments for his wife, Radha Yadav. Get the full breakdown of the legal ruling, fine distributions, and Rajpal Yadav's statements regarding his severe financial setbacks here.

title: 15 killed in Philippines landslides as Typhoon Bavi wreaks havoc
content: Typhoon Bavi wreaked havoc in the Philippines, leading to tragic landslides that claimed the lives of at least fifteen people. The approaching storm caused significant evacuations and transport disruptions, as Taiwan braced for severe flooding and landslides, resulting in widespread school and business closures. Although weakening, the typhoon was poised to impact northern and eastern Taiwan before heading towards eastern China after affecting regions in Taiwan and Japan.

title: Government pushes to operationalise capital’s second medical college
content: The government argues that the second medical college would ease the burden on Thiruvananthapuram Government Medical College, which is overwhelmed by the sheer number of patients, with no more room for development

title: CUK launches two four-year UG honours programmes
content: Candidates who have appeared for the CUET (UG) conducted by the National Testing Agency can apply online through the university website until July 17

title: Women’s coalition demands delinking of one-third quota from delimitation
content: The National Coalition for Women’s Reservation announced a nationwide programme to build public pressure for the immediate implementation of the quota, including joint protests and webinars

title: Opposition warns of State-wide stir if govt. fails to withdraw UAPA case against YouTuber Ravan by July 17
content: At a roundtable organised by the CPI, the speakers question the invoking of anti-terror provisions, alleging that the government is targeting critics to suppress dissent

title: District administration initiates plans to develop Kasaragod coastal tourism circuit
content: The project aims to transform the coastal stretch, including Nellikunnu and Kasaba harbour, into a major tourism hub

title: Karnataka should fight to increase its share from Godavari-Krishna-Cauvery river linking project, says Union Minister H.D. Kumaraswamy
content: Karnataka will get only a meagre quantum of water from the project, as is currently conceived, and the State should fight for its fair share, argued the Union Minister

title: Anganwadi workers seek ₹26,000 monthly wage, social security benefits
content: Workers stage protests outside Collectorates across Andhra Pradesh, warn of statewide siege on July 31 if demands remain unmet

title: Anganwadi workers demand salary hike and social security, stage protest in Ongole
content: Union leaders demand minimum monthly wage of ₹26,000 and the implementation of all social security schemes, including pension, PF, ESI, gratuity, and paid medical leave

title: No scaling down Mysuru Dasara, says D.K. Shivakumar
content: An executive committee will be constituted shortly to oversee the arrangements, and the govt. will also approach the Centre for permission to organise an air show as part of the festivities

title: Congress urges NDA States to oppose VBSA Bill on higher education
content: Congress leader Jairam Ramesh says proposed law centralises powers, weakens federal principles and reduces university autonomy; urges Andhra Pradesh CM to ‘stand up boldly’ if he believed the Bill harmed States’ interests

title: Kerala Agricultural University releases five new high-yielding crop varieties
content: New chilli, brahmi, vetiver, ginger and turmeric varieties promise higher yields, better quality and improved farm income

title: Talks to resolve nurses’ strike in Kannur end without breakthrough
content: Indian Nurses Association alleges talks failed after the CITU and BMS demanded that any wage revision for striking nurses be extended to employees of all 36 private hospitals in district

title: Assembly elections likely in mid-2029 after delimitation, Congress to retain power: Chief Minister A. Revanth Reddy
content: He forecast that the Congress would win 117 Assembly seats in the next elections and form the government for a second successive term

title: YSRCP using social media influencers to foment  caste and religious discord, alleges JSP
content: The opposition party’s decision to provide legal assistance to YouTuber Ravan, who is currently in judicial custody, exposes the nexus between them, allege JSP leaders, exhorting Dalit and Christian youths not to fall for such politically motivated campaigns

title: Ayyappa Sangamam will not be organised again, says TDB president
content: K. Jayakumar says Ayyappa does not need publicity; TDB decides to make financial transactions at Sabarimala  digital to ensure transparency; more rooms will be available to pilgrims for booking

title: SIR: 30% of Telangana’s electorate may face scrutiny due to anomalies in electoral rolls
content: The electors who are unmapped and whose information has anomalies would receive notices from the EROs after the completion of distribution and collection of forms

title: International experts launch natural farming model at Guntur school
content: Pilot 2,000 sq. m. project aims to promote food and nutrition security and inspire students to take up sustainable agriculture

title: 34-metre cycling track stretch removed in Narsingi for U-turn to decongest traffic flow
content: Traffic police expect the new underpass link to reduce congestion by around 30%, but cyclists say repeated removal of dedicated cycling infrastructure sets a worrying precedent

title: Google challenges order in Hindware trademark case, HC issues notice
content: Senior advocate Abhishek Singhvi sought interim stay of the direction to pay damages; he argued that the single Bench’s judgment was not in line with the earlier decisions and globally acceptable practices

title: Calcutta High Court asks Abhishek Banerjee to submit voice sample by July 15 in intimidatory speech case
content: The court directed police authorities to ensure that Banerjee is not subjected to egg-pelting or any other harassment when he appears before the court or the investigating agency

title: Benefits given to dead Gruha Lakshmi beneficiaries to be recovered after verification
content: The Ballari District Guarantee Schemes Authority Chairman says ₹2.29 crore has been credited to accounts of 2,224 deceased beneficiaries

title: CJI-led Bench to hear pleas on Ram Temple donation embezzlement case on July 13
content: A petition filed by Rashtriya Janata Dal (RJD) MP Sudhakar Singh to provide and publish complete financial details of the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust, including foreign contributions, is listed along with two other pleas

title: Owaisi demands Telangana government to issue Permanent Residence Certificates to help electors in SIR process
content: Hyderabad MP says Karanataka issues PRC certificates through a government order under the Karnataka Sakala Services Act, 2011.

title: Adani Group, CEE-USA Host RSI India 2026 Valedictory Ceremony At IISc Bengaluru
content: Tanishq, a student participating in the prestigious initiative, shared his experience during the valedictory ceremony.

title: Kerala Student Killed In Uzbekistan, Family Claims Conversion Attempt, Torture
content: The FIR was filed at Haripad police station on Wednesday after the victim's parents approached the Alappuzha District Police Chief, officials said. A postmortem examination was also conducted at Alappuzha Medical College as part of the investigation.

title: 27-Year-Old Dies After Falling Into Deep Open Drain In Noida
content: According to police, he was 28 years old and a resident of Village Chaura in Noida's Sector 22. He fell into a drain in Sector 57 while on his way to work in Sector 58.

title: Wayanad A Warning. Many Districts Share Similar Exposure To Landslide Hazards
content: India is among the four countries with the highest landslide risk in the world, with an estimated annual loss of more than one life per 100 sq km due to landslides.

title: "Write This Down": Revanth Reddy Predicts Congress Win In Telangana In 2029
content: He asserted that the Congress would secure over 115 of the 182 Assembly seats and urged journalists to "write this down" as a reflection of the party's confidence.

title: Assam's Rs 77,353 Crore Investment Plan To Expand Power Infrastructure
content: Assam has unveiled an ambitious roadmap to strengthen its power sector, proposing investments worth Rs 77,353 crore across a wide range of electricity generation, storage and transmission projects in the 2026-27 state Budget.

title: Opinion: Opinion | US-Iran MoU Collapse: Why The Strait Of Hormuz Is Back On The Brink
content: Why did the ceasefire fail? Could it have been prevented? The answer to many of these questions lies in the framing of the 14-Point MoU and the uneven playfield that it created.

title: Exclusive: Navy's Next Build-Up Takes Shape With 3 Warship Projects Worth Rs 1 Lakh Crore
content: Sources revealed to NDTV that the projects are going through various rounds of internal consultations and planning before moving forward to the defence acquisition process.

title: Head And Shoulders Outside: Man Nearly Sucked Out Of Flight As Window Detaches
content: The passenger was a tourist from Serbia who was travelling from Thessaloniki in Greece to Memmingen in Germany. He was hospitalised with friction burns but was otherwise in good condition.

title: 'May Arrest Or Kill Me': Hasina Says She'll Return To Bangladesh By December
content: Sheikh Hasina fled Bangladesh in 2024 after protests ended her 20 years as prime minister across multiple terms.

title: Maharashtra Raises Farm Loan Waiver Limit From Rs 50,000 To Rs 2 Lakh
content: The chief minister conceded that regular waivers could encourage borrowers to delay repayments and weaken cooperative banks' financial health.

title: Rajasthan Police To Stop Using 'Dalit', Replace Word With 'Scheduled Caste'
content: The Ministry of Information and Broadcasting had earlier issued an advisory to media organisations asking them to refrain from using the word "Dalit" in official broadcasts.

title: Not Reporting Child Sex Abuse A Crime, Headmaster To Be Tried: Supreme Court
content: The court set aside orders of the trial court and the Gauhati High Court that had discharged the headmistress from prosecution for allegedly failing to report the complaint of an eight-year-old student.

title: NDTV Investigation: No Single Cause Or Common Pattern In Bikaner's Maternal Health Crisis
content: Part 3 of NDTV's investigation into the series of maternal deaths and severe post-delivery complications examines a different pattern emerging at Bikaner's PBM Hospital

title: Petitioner Abuses Chief Justice, Throws Papers, Supreme Court Spares Him
content: The incident took place around 11 am before a partial working-day Bench of Justices KV Viswanathan and Alok Aradhe.

title: Man sentenced to death for killing 1.5-year-old nephew in UP's Firozabad
content: According to District Government Counsel Rajiv Upadhyay, the court convicted Viraj on Thursday and announced the sentence on Friday.

title: Opinion: Blog | Amaira, And The Daily Goodbye Every Parent Takes For Granted
content: I kept thinking about that moment every parent knows so well. The one where you watch your child disappear through the school gate, believing they will come home.

title: Hackers Exploit Software Flaw To Steal Rs 7.34 Crore From Gujarat Bank
content: CID officials said the hackers exploited a flaw in the banking software, which they suspect may have affected 14 to 15 other banks across the country.

title: Seeking Stamina Boosters, Thousands Fall Into Cyber Extortion Trap
content: Operating through sophisticated call centers, the gang exploited personal vulnerabilities by selling fake stamina-enhancing medicines online and then extorted the buyers by posing as law enforcement officials.

title: Daughter’s income certificate row: Karnataka High Court stays KPSC members’ resolution seeking resignation of chairman
content: The resolution was passed on June 19 in KPSC’s meeting, which was headed by senior-most member B. Prabhudev as the chairman had recused from chairing the meeting due to a conflict of interest

title: Telangana faces dry conditions with significant rainfall unlikely in July
content: Significant rainfall is likely only if weather systems develop closer to the Andhra Pradesh coast, says Telangana Development Planning Society (TGDPS) scientist

title: Kerala Forest Minister seeks report after tribal man threatens to kill himself over vehicle seizure
content: His earth mover was seized by the Forest department four-and-a-half years ago in connection with a case

title: Principal, warden suspended as 50 students fall ill in residential school
content: Administrative probe launched after children suffer severe indigestion following dinner at Morarji Desai Residential School in Dandoti

title: El Niño likely to hit oil sardine stocks in 2027, says CMFRI
content: The warming associated with El Niño is expected to intensify during October–December, and its impact is likely to be felt over the northern Indian Ocean by April–May 2027, says the CMFRI Director

title: Yathindra steps in to resolve UoM research scholars’ scholarship issue
content: The Minister has proposed a joint arrangement under which the Department of Social Welfare would provide scholarships to 58 students, while the University of Mysore would support the remaining 58 scholars

title: Science should reach society with integrity and transparency: Soumya Swaminathan
content: MSSRF Chairperson virtually addressed the IISc convocation; total of 1,452 PhD and Master’s students, and 118 undergraduate students received their degrees

title: Govt.’s intervention sought to resolve crisis gripping private bus sector
content: The State leadership of the Kerala State Motor and Engineering Labour Centre, affiliated with the Hind Mazdoor Sabha, says the Priyadarshini scheme, which offers free travel for women on KSRTC ordinary services, has triggered a crisis in the private bus sector

title: Awareness programme tomorrow to fight dowry among Banjaras
content: The Banjara community has been suffering from the practice of dowry from the ancient times, says leader of All India Banjara Seva Sangha

title: Nearly 80% of SIR enumeration forms digitised in Chittoor district, says Collector
content: BLOs and BLAs play a crucial role in the ongoing revision exercise, while weekly review meetings are being conducted with representatives of recognised political parties to ensure transparency, says the District Collector
